server.h(3) Library Functions Manual server.h(3)

NAME

server.h - Server-side handshake functions.

SYNOPSIS

Functions

Server Handshake Protocol



asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_server_start (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, acip_transport_t *transport)
Server: Start crypto handshake by sending public key. asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, acip_transport_t *transport, packet_type_t packet_type, const uint8_t *payload, size_t payload_len)
Server: Process client's public key and send auth challenge. asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_server_complete (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, acip_transport_t *transport, packet_type_t packet_type, const uint8_t *payload, size_t payload_len)
Server: Process auth response and complete handshake.

Legacy TCP Socket Wrappers (Backward Compatibility)
These wrappers maintain the old socket-based interface for TCP clients. They will be removed in Phase 5 when TCP client code is refactored.



asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_server_start_socket (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, socket_t client_socket)
Legacy wrapper: Start handshake using socket (TCP clients only). asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge_socket (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, socket_t client_socket)
Legacy wrapper: Auth challenge using socket (TCP clients only). asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_server_complete_socket (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, socket_t client_socket)
Legacy wrapper: Complete handshake using socket (TCP clients only).

Detailed Description

Server-side handshake functions.

Definition in file include/ascii-chat/crypto/handshake/server.h.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, acip_transport_t * transport, packet_type_t packet_type, const uint8_t * payload, size_t payload_len)

Server: Process client's public key and send auth challenge.

Parameters

ctx Handshake context (must be in CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE state)
transport ACIP transport to send on
packet_type Packet type received (should be PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_RESP or PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_NO_ENCRYPTION)
payload Received packet payload
payload_len Length of received payload

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure

Server processes client's KEY_EXCHANGE_RESP packet and sends AUTH_CHALLENGE. Computes shared secret and generates authentication challenge nonce.

Note

Packet must be received by ACIP handler before calling this function

State transition: CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE -> CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING

Definition at line 139 of file lib/crypto/handshake/server.c.

141                                                                              {
142   if (!ctx || ctx->state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE) {
143     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid state: ctx=%p, state=%d", ctx, ctx ? ctx->state : -1);
144   }
145   if (!transport) {
146     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "transport is NULL");
147   }
148 
149   // Note: Packet already received by ACIP handler
150   int result;
151 
152   // Check if client sent NO_ENCRYPTION response
153   if (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_NO_ENCRYPTION) {
154 
155     // Send AUTH_FAILED to inform client (though they already know)
156     auth_failure_packet_t failure = {0};
157     failure.reason_flags = 0; // No specific auth failure, just encryption mismatch
158     int send_result =
159         packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED, &failure, sizeof(failure), 0);
160     if (send_result != 0) {
161       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send AUTH_FAILED packet");
162     }
163 
164     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "SECURITY: Client sent NO_ENCRYPTION response - encryption mode "
165                                    "mismatch. Server requires encryption, but "
166                                    "client has --no-encrypt. Use matching encryption settings on "
167                                    "both client and server");
168   }
169 
170   // Verify packet type
171   if (packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_RESP) {
172     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Expected KEY_EXCHANGE_RESPONSE, got packet type %d", packet_type);
173   }
174 
175   // Verify payload size - client can send either simple or authenticated format
176   // Simple: kex_public_key_size bytes
177   // Authenticated: public_key_size + client_auth_key_size + client_sig_size bytes
178   size_t simple_size = ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size;
179   // Ed25519 public key is always 32 bytes
180   // Ed25519 signature is always 64 bytes
181   size_t authenticated_size = ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size + ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE + ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE;
182 
183   bool client_sent_identity = false;
184   uint8_t *client_ephemeral_key = SAFE_MALLOC(ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size, uint8_t *);
185   uint8_t *client_identity_key = SAFE_MALLOC(ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE, uint8_t *);
186   uint8_t *client_signature = SAFE_MALLOC(ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE, uint8_t *);
187 
188   if (!client_ephemeral_key || !client_identity_key || !client_signature) {
189     if (client_ephemeral_key)
190       SAFE_FREE(client_ephemeral_key);
191     if (client_identity_key)
192       SAFE_FREE(client_identity_key);
193     if (client_signature)
194       SAFE_FREE(client_signature);
195     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate memory for client keys");
196   }
197 
198   // Validate packet size using session parameters
199   asciichat_error_t validation_result =
200       crypto_handshake_validate_packet_size(ctx, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_RESP, payload_len);
201   if (validation_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
202     // Payload ownership remains with caller
203     SAFE_FREE(client_ephemeral_key);
204     SAFE_FREE(client_identity_key);
205     SAFE_FREE(client_signature);
206     return validation_result;
207   }
208 
209   // Handle both authenticated and non-authenticated responses
210   // authenticated_size is now a minimum - packet may be larger with GPG key ID
211   if (payload_len >= authenticated_size) {
212     // Authenticated format (may have optional GPG key ID):
213     // [ephemeral:kex_size][identity:auth_size][signature:sig_size][gpg_key_id_len:1][gpg_key_id:0-16]
214     memcpy(client_ephemeral_key, payload, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size);
215     memcpy(client_identity_key, payload + ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size, ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE);
216     memcpy(client_signature, payload + ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size + ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE,
217            ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE);
218     client_sent_identity = true;
219     ctx->client_sent_identity = true;
220 
221     // Extract GPG key ID if present
222     const char *client_gpg_key_id = NULL;
223     char gpg_key_id_buffer[41] = {0};
224     size_t gpg_offset = ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size + ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE + ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE;
225     if (payload_len > gpg_offset) {
226       uint8_t gpg_key_id_len = payload[gpg_offset];
227       if (gpg_key_id_len > 0 && gpg_key_id_len <= 40 && payload_len >= gpg_offset + 1 + gpg_key_id_len) {
228         memcpy(gpg_key_id_buffer, payload + gpg_offset + 1, gpg_key_id_len);
229         gpg_key_id_buffer[gpg_key_id_len] = '\0';
230         client_gpg_key_id = gpg_key_id_buffer;
231         log_debug("Extracted client GPG key ID from KEY_EXCHANGE_RESPONSE: %s", client_gpg_key_id);
232       }
233     }
234 
235     // Check if client sent a null identity key (all zeros)
236     bool client_has_null_identity = true;
237     for (size_t i = 0; i < ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE; i++) {
238       if (client_identity_key[i] != 0) {
239         client_has_null_identity = false;
240         break;
241       }
242     }
243 
244     if (client_has_null_identity) {
245       // Client has no identity key - this is allowed for servers without client authentication
246       log_debug("Client sent null identity key - no client authentication required");
247       client_sent_identity = false;
248       ctx->client_sent_identity = false;
249       log_warn("Client connected without identity authentication");
250     } else {
251       // Client has a real identity key
252       // If server didn't specify --client-keys, skip signature verification
253       // (server doesn't care about client identity verification)
254       if (!ctx->require_client_auth) {
255         log_info("Skipping client signature verification (no --client-keys specified)");
256         log_warn("Connection is encrypted but client identity is NOT verified");
257       } else {
258         // Verify client's signature
259         log_debug("Verifying client's signature");
260         // Pass client's GPG key ID if available
261         if (ed25519_verify_signature(client_identity_key, client_ephemeral_key, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size,
262                                      client_signature, client_gpg_key_id) != 0) {
263           // Send AUTH_FAILED with specific reason
264           auth_failure_packet_t failure = {0};
265           failure.reason_flags = AUTH_FAIL_SIGNATURE_INVALID;
266           int send_result =
267               packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED, &failure, sizeof(failure), 0);
268           if (send_result != 0) {
269             return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send AUTH_FAILED packet");
270           }
271 
272           return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Client signature verification FAILED - rejecting connection");
273         }
274       }
275     }
276 
277     // Store the verified client identity for whitelist checking (only if client has identity)
278     if (client_sent_identity) {
279       ctx->client_ed25519_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
280       memcpy(ctx->client_ed25519_key.key, client_identity_key, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size);
281     }
282   } else if (payload_len == simple_size) {
283     // Non-authenticated format: [ephemeral:public_key_size] only
284     log_debug("Client sent non-authenticated response (%zu bytes)", payload_len);
285     memcpy(client_ephemeral_key, payload, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size);
286     client_sent_identity = false;
287     ctx->client_sent_identity = false;
288     log_warn("Client connected without identity authentication");
289   } else {
290     // Payload ownership remains with caller
291     SAFE_FREE(client_ephemeral_key);
292     SAFE_FREE(client_identity_key);
293     SAFE_FREE(client_signature);
294     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
295                      "Invalid client key response size: %zu bytes (expected %zu for "
296                      "authenticated or %zu for simple)",
297                      payload_len, authenticated_size, simple_size);
298   }
299 
300   // Extract pointers for compatibility with existing code
301   const uint8_t *client_x25519 = client_ephemeral_key;
302   const uint8_t *client_ed25519 = client_sent_identity ? client_identity_key : NULL;
303 
304   // Check client Ed25519 key against whitelist if client provided one and
305   // whitelist is enabled
306   if (client_sent_identity && ctx->require_client_auth && ctx->client_whitelist && ctx->num_whitelisted_clients > 0) {
307     bool key_found = false;
308 
309     // Debug: print client's Ed25519 identity key
310     char client_ed25519_hex[HEX_STRING_SIZE_32];
311     for (int i = 0; i < ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE; i++) {
312       safe_snprintf(client_ed25519_hex + i * 2, 3, "%02x", client_ed25519[i]);
313     }
314     log_debug("Client Ed25519 identity key: %s", client_ed25519_hex);
315 
316     // Compare against whitelist (direct Ed25519 comparison - no conversion!)
317     for (size_t i = 0; i < ctx->num_whitelisted_clients; i++) {
318       // Debug: print whitelist Ed25519 key
319       char whitelist_ed25519_hex[HEX_STRING_SIZE_32];
320       for (int j = 0; j < ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE; j++) {
321         safe_snprintf(whitelist_ed25519_hex + j * 2, 3, "%02x", ctx->client_whitelist[i].key[j]);
322       }
323       log_debug("Whitelist[%zu] Ed25519 key: %s", i, whitelist_ed25519_hex);
324 
325       // Direct comparison of Ed25519 keys (constant-time to prevent timing
326       // attacks)
327       if (sodium_memcmp(client_ed25519, ctx->client_whitelist[i].key, ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE) == 0) {
328         key_found = true;
329         ctx->client_ed25519_key_verified = true;
330 
331         // Store the client's Ed25519 key for signature verification
332         memcpy(&ctx->client_ed25519_key, &ctx->client_whitelist[i], sizeof(public_key_t));
333 
334         log_debug("Client Ed25519 key authorized (whitelist entry %zu)", i);
335         if (strlen(ctx->client_whitelist[i].comment) > 0) {
336           log_info("Client identity: %s", ctx->client_whitelist[i].comment);
337         }
338         break;
339       }
340     }
341 
342     if (!key_found) {
343       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "Client Ed25519 key not in whitelist - rejecting connection");
344       // Don't send AUTH_FAILED here - wait until server_complete
345       // Just mark that the key was rejected
346       ctx->client_ed25519_key_verified = false;
347     }
348   } else if (client_sent_identity) {
349     // No whitelist checking - just store the client's Ed25519 key for later
350     // (Already stored at line 313-314, but keep this for clarity)
351     ctx->client_ed25519_key_verified = false;
352   }
353 
354   // Set peer's X25519 encryption key - this also derives the shared secret
355   crypto_result_t crypto_result = crypto_set_peer_public_key(&ctx->crypto_ctx, client_x25519);
356   if (crypto_result != CRYPTO_OK) {
357     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to set peer public key and derive shared secret: %s",
358                      crypto_result_to_string(crypto_result));
359   }
360 
361   // Clean up allocated memory
362   SAFE_FREE(client_ephemeral_key);
363   SAFE_FREE(client_identity_key);
364   SAFE_FREE(client_signature);
365 
366   // Do authentication challenge if client provided identity key OR server
367   // requires password
368   if (client_sent_identity || ctx->crypto_ctx.has_password || ctx->require_client_auth) {
369     // Generate nonce and store it in the context
370     crypto_result = crypto_generate_nonce(ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_nonce);
371     if (crypto_result != CRYPTO_OK) {
372       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to generate nonce: %s", crypto_result_to_string(crypto_result));
373     }
374 
375     // Prepare AUTH_CHALLENGE packet: 1 byte flags + auth_challenge_size byte nonce
376     size_t challenge_packet_size = AUTH_CHALLENGE_FLAGS_SIZE + ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size;
377     uint8_t challenge_packet[1 + HMAC_SHA256_SIZE]; // Maximum size buffer
378     uint8_t auth_flags = 0;
379 
380     // Set flags based on server requirements
381     if (ctx->crypto_ctx.has_password) {
382       auth_flags |= AUTH_REQUIRE_PASSWORD;
383     }
384     if (ctx->require_client_auth) {
385       auth_flags |= AUTH_REQUIRE_CLIENT_KEY;
386     }
387 
388     challenge_packet[0] = auth_flags;
389     memcpy(challenge_packet + 1, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_nonce, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size);
390 
391     // Send AUTH_CHALLENGE with flags + nonce (challenge_packet_size bytes)
392     result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_CHALLENGE, challenge_packet,
393                                        challenge_packet_size, 0);
394     if (result != 0) {
395       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send AUTH_CHALLENGE packet");
396     }
397 
398     ctx->state = CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING;
399   } else {
400     // No authentication needed - skip to completion
401     log_debug("Skipping authentication (no password and client has no identity key)");
402 
403     // Send HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE immediately
404     result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE, NULL, 0, 0);
405     if (result != 0) {
406       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE packet");
407     }
408 
409     ctx->state = CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY;
410     ctx->crypto_ctx.handshake_complete = true; // Mark crypto context as ready for rekeying
411     log_debug("Crypto handshake completed successfully (no authentication)");
412   }
413 
414   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
415 }




References ASCIICHAT_OK, AUTH_CHALLENGE_FLAGS_SIZE,
    crypto_context_t::auth_challenge_size,
    AUTH_FAIL_SIGNATURE_INVALID, crypto_context_t::auth_nonce,
    AUTH_REQUIRE_CLIENT_KEY, AUTH_REQUIRE_PASSWORD,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_ed25519_key,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_ed25519_key_verified,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_sent_identity,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_whitelist,
    public_key_t::comment, crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx,
    crypto_generate_nonce(), CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING,
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE, CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY,
    crypto_handshake_validate_packet_size(), CRYPTO_OK,
    crypto_result_to_string(), crypto_set_peer_public_key(),
    ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE, ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE,
    ed25519_verify_signature(), ERROR_CRYPTO,
    ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_NETWORK,
    ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, crypto_context_t::handshake_complete,
    crypto_context_t::has_password, HEX_STRING_SIZE_32,
    HMAC_SHA256_SIZE, public_key_t::key, KEY_TYPE_ED25519,
    log_debug, log_info, log_warn,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::num_whitelisted_clients,
    packet_send_via_transport(),
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_CHALLENGE,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_RESP,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_NO_ENCRYPTION,
    crypto_context_t::public_key_size,
    auth_failure_packet_t::reason_flags,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::require_client_auth, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, safe_snprintf(), SET_ERRNO,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::state, and public_key_t::type.


Referenced by
    crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge_socket().






asciichat_error_t
  crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge_socket
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, socket_t client_socket)


Legacy wrapper: Auth challenge using socket (TCP clients
  only).


Deprecated


Use crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge() with
  acip_transport_t instead


Definition at line 647 of file
    lib/crypto/handshake/server.c.


648                                                                                         {
649   // Receive packet using old method
650   packet_type_t packet_type;
651   uint8_t *payload = NULL;
652   size_t payload_len = 0;
653   int result = receive_packet(client_socket, &packet_type, (void **)&payload, &payload_len);
654   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
655     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to receive KEY_EXCHANGE_RESPONSE packet");
656   }
657 
658   // Create temporary TCP transport
659   acip_transport_t *temp_transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("crypto_handshake_temp_socket", client_socket, NULL);
660   if (!temp_transport) {
661     // Payload will be freed below since we own it from receive_packet()
662     if (payload) {
663       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
664     }
665     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create temporary transport");
666   }
667 
668   // Call new function
669   asciichat_error_t handshake_result =
670       crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge(ctx, temp_transport, packet_type, payload, payload_len);
671 
672   // Free payload (handshake function makes copies of what it needs)
673   // We own the payload from receive_packet(), so we must free it
674   if (payload) {
675     buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
676   }
677 
678   // Destroy temporary transport
679   acip_transport_destroy(temp_transport);
680 
681   return handshake_result;
682 }






References acip_tcp_transport_create(),
    acip_transport_destroy(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    buffer_pool_free(), crypto_handshake_server_auth_challenge(),
    ERROR_NETWORK, receive_packet(), and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by acds_client_handler(), and
    server_crypto_handshake().






asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_server_complete
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, acip_transport_t * transport,
  packet_type_t packet_type, const uint8_t * payload, size_t
  payload_len)


Server: Process auth response and complete handshake.


Parameters


ctx Handshake context (must be in
  CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING state)


transport ACIP transport to send on


packet_type Packet type received (should be
  PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_RESPONSE)


payload Received packet payload


payload_len Length of received payload


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Server processes client's AUTH_RESPONSE packet and sends
    SERVER_AUTH_RESP. Verifies authentication HMAC or signature and completes
    handshake.




Note


Packet must be received by ACIP handler before calling
  this function



State transition: CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING ->
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY




Definition at line 417 of file
    lib/crypto/handshake/server.c.


419                                                                        {
420   if (!ctx || ctx->state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING) {
421     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid state: ctx=%p, state=%d", ctx, ctx ? ctx->state : -1);
422   }
423   if (!transport) {
424     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "transport is NULL");
425   }
426 
427   // Note: Packet already received by ACIP handler
428   int result;
429 
430   // Verify packet type
431   if (packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_RESPONSE) {
432     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Expected AUTH_RESPONSE, got packet type %d", packet_type);
433   }
434 
435   // Verify password if required
436   if (ctx->crypto_ctx.has_password) {
437     // Validate packet size using session parameters
438     asciichat_error_t validation_result =
439         crypto_handshake_validate_packet_size(ctx, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_RESPONSE, payload_len);
440     if (validation_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
441       return validation_result;
442     }
443 
444     // Ensure shared secret is derived before password verification
445     // This is critical for password HMAC verification which binds to the shared secret
446     if (!ctx->crypto_ctx.key_exchange_complete) {
447       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Password authentication failed - key exchange not complete");
448 
449       // Send AUTH_FAILED with specific reason
450       auth_failure_packet_t failure = {0};
451       failure.reason_flags = AUTH_FAIL_PASSWORD_INCORRECT;
452       if (ctx->require_client_auth) {
453         failure.reason_flags |= AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REQUIRED;
454       }
455       packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED, &failure, sizeof(failure), 0);
456       return ERROR_NETWORK;
457     }
458 
459     // Verify password HMAC (binds to DH shared_secret to prevent MITM)
460     log_debug("Verifying password HMAC: has_password=%d, key_exchange_complete=%d", ctx->crypto_ctx.has_password,
461               ctx->crypto_ctx.key_exchange_complete);
462     if (!payload) {
463       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Payload is NULL in password authentication");
464       return ERROR_CRYPTO;
465     }
466     if (!crypto_verify_auth_response(&ctx->crypto_ctx, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_nonce, payload)) {
467       log_debug("Password HMAC verification failed");
468       // Enhanced error message when both password and whitelist are required
469       if (ctx->require_client_auth) {
470         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO,
471                   "Password authentication failed - incorrect password (server also requires whitelisted client key)");
472       } else {
473         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Password authentication failed - incorrect password");
474       }
475 
476       // Send AUTH_FAILED with specific reason
477       auth_failure_packet_t failure = {0};
478       failure.reason_flags = AUTH_FAIL_PASSWORD_INCORRECT;
479       if (ctx->require_client_auth) {
480         failure.reason_flags |= AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REQUIRED;
481       }
482       packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED, &failure, sizeof(failure), 0);
483       return ERROR_NETWORK;
484     }
485 
486     // Extract client challenge nonce for mutual authentication
487     // Use ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size and ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size (negotiated during handshake)
488     memcpy(ctx->client_challenge_nonce, payload + ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size);
489     log_info("Password authentication successful");
490   } else {
491     // Ed25519 signature auth (payload format: signature + client_nonce + gpg_key_id_len + gpg_key_id)
492     size_t expected_min_signature_size =
493         ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size + ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size + 1; // +1 for gpg_key_id_len byte
494     size_t expected_password_size = ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size + ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size;
495     if (!payload) {
496       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Payload is NULL in signature authentication");
497       return ERROR_CRYPTO;
498     }
499     if (payload_len >= expected_min_signature_size) {
500       // Signature + client nonce + optional GPG key ID - VERIFY the signature on the challenge nonce
501       const uint8_t *signature = payload;
502       const uint8_t *client_nonce = payload + ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size;
503 
504       // Extract GPG key ID if present
505       size_t gpg_offset = ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size + ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size;
506       uint8_t gpg_key_id_len = payload[gpg_offset];
507       const char *client_gpg_key_id = NULL;
508       char gpg_key_id_buffer[41] = {0};
509 
510       if (gpg_key_id_len > 0 && gpg_key_id_len <= 40) {
511         // Validate packet has enough bytes for GPG key ID
512         if (payload_len >= gpg_offset + 1 + gpg_key_id_len) {
513           memcpy(gpg_key_id_buffer, payload + gpg_offset + 1, gpg_key_id_len);
514           gpg_key_id_buffer[gpg_key_id_len] = '\0';
515           client_gpg_key_id = gpg_key_id_buffer;
516           log_debug("Extracted client GPG key ID from AUTH_RESPONSE: %s", client_gpg_key_id);
517         }
518       }
519 
520       // Actually verify the Ed25519/GPG signature on the challenge nonce
521       // This was missing, allowing authentication bypass
522       if (ctx->client_ed25519_key_verified) {
523         // Use unified verification function that handles both Ed25519 and GPG keys
524         // This matches the KEY_EXCHANGE verification path
525         log_debug("Verifying %s signature on challenge nonce",
526                   ctx->client_ed25519_key.type == KEY_TYPE_GPG ? "GPG" : "Ed25519");
527 
528         asciichat_error_t verify_result = ed25519_verify_signature(
529             ctx->client_ed25519_key.key, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_nonce, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size, signature,
530             client_gpg_key_id // GPG key ID for fallback verification
531         );
532 
533         if (verify_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
534           auth_failure_packet_t failure = {0};
535           failure.reason_flags = AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REJECTED;
536           SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "%s signature verification failed on challenge nonce",
537                     ctx->client_ed25519_key.type == KEY_TYPE_GPG ? "GPG" : "Ed25519");
538           packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED, &failure, sizeof(failure), 0);
539           return ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH;
540         }
541         log_debug("%s signature on challenge nonce verified successfully",
542                   ctx->client_ed25519_key.type == KEY_TYPE_GPG ? "GPG" : "Ed25519");
543       }
544 
545       memcpy(ctx->client_challenge_nonce, client_nonce, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size);
546     } else if (payload_len == expected_password_size) {
547       // Just client nonce (legacy or password-only mode without password enabled)
548       // Use ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size and ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size (negotiated during handshake)
549       memcpy(ctx->client_challenge_nonce, payload + ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size);
550     } else {
551       // Validate packet size using session parameters
552       asciichat_error_t validation_result =
553           crypto_handshake_validate_packet_size(ctx, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_RESPONSE, payload_len);
554       if (validation_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
555         return validation_result;
556       }
557     }
558   }
559 
560   // Verify client key if required (whitelist)
561   if (ctx->require_client_auth) {
562     if (!ctx->client_ed25519_key_verified) {
563       // Send AUTH_FAILED with specific reason
564       auth_failure_packet_t failure = {0};
565 
566       // Check if client provided a key but it wasn't in whitelist
567       if (ctx->client_sent_identity) {
568         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "Client key authentication failed - your key is not in the server's whitelist");
569         failure.reason_flags = AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REJECTED;
570       } else {
571         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "Client key authentication failed - client did not provide a key");
572         failure.reason_flags = AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REQUIRED;
573       }
574 
575       if (ctx->crypto_ctx.has_password) {
576         // Password was verified, but key was not
577         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "Note: Password was correct, but client key is required");
578       }
579       packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED, &failure, sizeof(failure), 0);
580       return ERROR_NETWORK;
581     }
582     log_info("Client key authentication successful (whitelist verified)");
583     if (strlen(ctx->client_ed25519_key.comment) > 0) {
584       log_info("Authenticated client: %s", ctx->client_ed25519_key.comment);
585     }
586   }
587 
588   // Send SERVER_AUTH_RESPONSE with server's HMAC for mutual authentication
589   // Bind to DH shared_secret to prevent MITM (even if attacker knows password)
590   // Allocate buffer using negotiated hmac_size (should match AUTH_HMAC_SIZE)
591   uint8_t server_hmac[HMAC_SHA256_SIZE]; // Maximum size, actual size is ctx->hmac_size
592   crypto_result_t crypto_result =
593       crypto_compute_auth_response(&ctx->crypto_ctx, ctx->client_challenge_nonce, server_hmac);
594   if (crypto_result != CRYPTO_OK) {
595     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to compute server HMAC for mutual authentication: %s",
596               crypto_result_to_string(crypto_result));
597     return ERROR_NETWORK;
598   }
599 
600   log_debug("Sending SERVER_AUTH_RESPONSE packet with server HMAC (%u bytes) "
601             "for mutual authentication",
602             ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size);
603   result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_SERVER_AUTH_RESP, server_hmac,
604                                      ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size, 0);
605   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
606     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send SERVER_AUTH_RESPONSE packet");
607     return ERROR_NETWORK;
608   }
609 
610   ctx->state = CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY;
611   log_debug("Crypto handshake completed successfully (mutual authentication)");
612 
613   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
614 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    crypto_context_t::auth_challenge_size,
    AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REJECTED, AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REQUIRED,
    AUTH_FAIL_PASSWORD_INCORRECT, crypto_context_t::auth_nonce,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_challenge_nonce,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_ed25519_key,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_ed25519_key_verified,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_sent_identity,
    public_key_t::comment, crypto_compute_auth_response(),
    crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx,
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING, CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY,
    crypto_handshake_validate_packet_size(), CRYPTO_OK,
    crypto_result_to_string(), crypto_verify_auth_response(),
    ed25519_verify_signature(), ERROR_CRYPTO,
    ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_NETWORK,
    ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, crypto_context_t::has_password,
    HMAC_SHA256_SIZE, crypto_context_t::hmac_size,
    public_key_t::key, crypto_context_t::key_exchange_complete,
    KEY_TYPE_GPG, log_debug, log_info,
    packet_send_via_transport(), PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_RESPONSE,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_SERVER_AUTH_RESP,
    auth_failure_packet_t::reason_flags,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::require_client_auth, SET_ERRNO,
    crypto_context_t::signature_size,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::state, and public_key_t::type.


Referenced by
  crypto_handshake_server_complete_socket().






asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_server_complete_socket
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, socket_t client_socket)


Legacy wrapper: Complete handshake using socket (TCP clients
    only).


Deprecated


Use crypto_handshake_server_complete() with
  acip_transport_t instead


Definition at line 687 of file
    lib/crypto/handshake/server.c.


687                                                                                                                    {
688   // Receive packet using old method
689   packet_type_t packet_type = 0;
690   uint8_t *payload = NULL;
691   size_t payload_len = 0;
692   int result = receive_packet(client_socket, &packet_type, (void **)&payload, &payload_len);
693   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
694     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to receive AUTH_RESPONSE packet");
695   }
696 
697   // Create temporary TCP transport
698   acip_transport_t *temp_transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("crypto_handshake_temp_socket", client_socket, NULL);
699   if (!temp_transport) {
700     // Payload will be freed below since we own it from receive_packet()
701     if (payload) {
702       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
703     }
704     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create temporary transport");
705   }
706 
707   // Call new function
708   asciichat_error_t handshake_result =
709       crypto_handshake_server_complete(ctx, temp_transport, packet_type, payload, payload_len);
710 
711   // Free payload (handshake function makes copies of what it needs)
712   // We own the payload from receive_packet(), so we must free it
713   if (payload) {
714     buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
715   }
716 
717   // Destroy temporary transport
718   acip_transport_destroy(temp_transport);
719 
720   return handshake_result;
721 }






References acip_tcp_transport_create(),
    acip_transport_destroy(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    buffer_pool_free(), crypto_handshake_server_complete(),
    ERROR_NETWORK, receive_packet(), and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by acds_client_handler(), and
    server_crypto_handshake().






asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_server_start
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, acip_transport_t *
  transport)


Server: Start crypto handshake by sending public key.


Parameters


ctx Handshake context (must be in
  CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT state)


transport ACIP transport to send on


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Server initiates handshake by sending KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT packet.
    Supports both simple and authenticated formats based on server identity
  key.




Note


Packet formats:

    
  
  • Simple: [ephemeral_key:public_key_size] (when server has no identity
    key)
    • 
  
  • Authenticated:
      [ephemeral_key:public_key_size][identity_key:auth_public_key_size][signature:signature_size]
      (when server has Ed25519 identity key)
    • 



State transition: CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT ->
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE




<X25519 key exchange (Curve25519)




<Ed25519 authentication (Edwards-curve signatures)




<XSalsa20-Poly1305 authenticated encryption


Definition at line 20 of file
    lib/crypto/handshake/server.c.


20                                                                                                               {
21   log_debug("[HANDSHAKE_START] ===== ENTRY: crypto_handshake_server_start called =====");
22   log_debug("[HANDSHAKE_START] ctx=%p, transport=%p", (void *)ctx, (void *)transport);
23 
24   if (!ctx || ctx->state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT) {
25     log_error("[HANDSHAKE_START] FAILED: Invalid state - ctx=%p, state=%d", (void *)ctx, ctx ? (int)ctx->state : -1);
26     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid state: ctx=%p, state=%d", (void *)ctx, ctx ? (int)ctx->state : -1);
27   }
28   log_debug("[HANDSHAKE_START] State check OK: state=%d (CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT)", ctx->state);
29 
30   int result;
31 
32   // Step 1: Send CRYPTO_PARAMETERS packet first (algorithm negotiation)
33   // This tells the client what key sizes and algorithms we're using
34   log_debug("[HANDSHAKE_START] STEP 1: Sending CRYPTO_PARAMETERS to client");
35   crypto_parameters_packet_t params;
36   memset(&params, 0, sizeof(params));
37 
38   params.selected_kex = KEX_ALGO_X25519;
39   params.selected_auth = AUTH_ALGO_ED25519;
40   params.selected_cipher = CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305;
41   params.verification_enabled = ctx->require_client_auth ? 1 : 0;
42   params.kex_public_key_size = htons(ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size);
43   params.auth_public_key_size = htons(ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE);
44   params.signature_size = htons(ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE);
45   params.shared_secret_size = htons(CRYPTO_SHARED_KEY_SIZE);
46   params.nonce_size = XSALSA20_NONCE_SIZE;
47   params.mac_size = POLY1305_MAC_SIZE;
48   params.hmac_size = 32;  // SHA256 output size
49 
50   result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_PARAMETERS, (uint8_t *)&params, sizeof(params), 0);
51   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
52     log_error("[HANDSHAKE_START] FAILED: Could not send CRYPTO_PARAMETERS packet");
53     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send CRYPTO_PARAMETERS packet");
54   }
55   log_debug("[HANDSHAKE_START] CRYPTO_PARAMETERS sent successfully");
56 
57   // Calculate packet size based on negotiated crypto parameters
58   size_t expected_packet_size =
59       ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size + ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size + ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size;
60 
61   log_debug("SERVER_KEY_EXCHANGE: kex_size=%u, auth_size=%u, sig_size=%u, expected_size=%zu",
62             ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size, ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size,
63             expected_packet_size);
64 
65   // Check if we have an identity key to send authenticated packet
66   if (ctx->server_private_key.type == KEY_TYPE_ED25519) {
67     // Extended packet format:
68     // [ephemeral_key:kex_size][identity_key:auth_size][signature:sig_size]
69     uint8_t *extended_packet;
70     extended_packet = SAFE_MALLOC(expected_packet_size, uint8_t *);
71     if (!extended_packet) {
72       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate memory for extended packet");
73     }
74 
75     // Copy ephemeral public key
76     memcpy(extended_packet, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size);
77 
78     // Copy identity public key
79     memcpy(extended_packet + ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size, ctx->server_private_key.public_key,
80            ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size);
81 
82     // DEBUG: Print identity key being sent
83     char hex[HEX_STRING_SIZE_32];
84     for (int i = 0; i < ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE; i++) {
85       safe_snprintf(hex + i * 2, 3, "%02x", ctx->server_private_key.public_key[i]);
86     }
87     hex[HEX_STRING_SIZE_32 - 1] = '\0';
88 
89     // Sign the ephemeral key with our identity key
90     log_debug("Signing ephemeral key with server identity key");
91 
92     // Log key details in debug mode
93     char hex_ephemeral[65], hex_identity[65];
94     for (int i = 0; i < 32; i++) {
95       safe_snprintf(hex_ephemeral + i * 2, 3, "%02x", ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key[i]);
96       safe_snprintf(hex_identity + i * 2, 3, "%02x", ctx->server_private_key.public_key[i]);
97     }
98     hex_ephemeral[64] = hex_identity[64] = '\0';
99     log_debug("SERVER: Ephemeral key (32 bytes): %s", hex_ephemeral);
100     log_debug("SERVER: Identity public key: %s", hex_identity);
101 
102     if (ed25519_sign_message(&ctx->server_private_key, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size,
103                              extended_packet + ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size +
104                                  ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size) != 0) {
105       SAFE_FREE(extended_packet);
106       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to sign ephemeral key with identity key");
107     }
108 
109     log_debug("Sending authenticated KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT (%zu bytes: ephemeral + "
110               "identity + signature)",
111               expected_packet_size);
112     log_debug("[HANDSHAKE_START] Calling packet_send_via_transport(type=%d, payload_len=%zu, 0)...",
113               PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT, expected_packet_size);
114     result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT, extended_packet,
115                                        expected_packet_size, 0);
116     log_debug("[HANDSHAKE_START] packet_send_via_transport returned %d", result);
117     SAFE_FREE(extended_packet);
118   } else {
119     // No identity key - send just the ephemeral key
120     log_debug("Sending simple KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT (%zu bytes: ephemeral key only)", ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size);
121     log_debug("[HANDSHAKE_START] Calling packet_send_via_transport(type=%d, payload_len=%zu, 0)...",
122               PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size);
123     result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key,
124                                        ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size, 0);
125     log_debug("[HANDSHAKE_START] packet_send_via_transport returned %d", result);
126   }
127 
128   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
129     log_error("[HANDSHAKE_START] FAILED: packet_send returned %d", result);
130     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT packet");
131   }
132   log_debug("[HANDSHAKE_START] ===== SUCCESS: KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT sent =====");
133 
134   ctx->state = CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE;
135 
136   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
137 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, AUTH_ALGO_ED25519,
    crypto_context_t::auth_public_key_size,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::auth_public_key_size,
    CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx, CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT,
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE, CRYPTO_SHARED_KEY_SIZE,
    ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE, ed25519_sign_message(),
    ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE, ERROR_CRYPTO,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_NETWORK,
    HEX_STRING_SIZE_32, crypto_parameters_packet_t::hmac_size,
    KEX_ALGO_X25519,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::kex_public_key_size,
    KEY_TYPE_ED25519, log_debug, log_error,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::mac_size,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::nonce_size,
    packet_send_via_transport(),
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_PARAMETERS, POLY1305_MAC_SIZE,
    crypto_context_t::public_key, private_key_t::public_key,
    crypto_context_t::public_key_size,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::require_client_auth, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, safe_snprintf(),
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::selected_auth,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::selected_cipher,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::selected_kex,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_private_key, SET_ERRNO,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::shared_secret_size,
    crypto_context_t::signature_size,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::signature_size,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::state, private_key_t::type,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::verification_enabled, and
    XSALSA20_NONCE_SIZE.


Referenced by crypto_handshake_server_start_socket().






asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_server_start_socket
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, socket_t client_socket)


Legacy wrapper: Start handshake using socket (TCP clients
  only).


Deprecated


Use crypto_handshake_server_start() with
  acip_transport_t instead


Definition at line 625 of file
    lib/crypto/handshake/server.c.


625                                                                                                                 {
626   if (!ctx) {
627     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Context cannot be NULL");
628   }
629 
630   // Create temporary TCP transport for handshake
631   acip_transport_t *temp_transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("crypto_handshake_temp_socket", client_socket, NULL);
632   if (!temp_transport) {
633     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create temporary transport");
634   }
635 
636   asciichat_error_t result = crypto_handshake_server_start(ctx, temp_transport);
637 
638   // Destroy temporary transport (socket remains open)
639   acip_transport_destroy(temp_transport);
640 
641   return result;
642 }






References acip_tcp_transport_create(),
    acip_transport_destroy(), crypto_handshake_server_start(),
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_NETWORK, and SET_ERRNO.

    ascii-chat
